twitter_thread: 85% of Autonomous LLM projects fail due to poor training data, with 40% using datasets smaller than 100k samples.
Lucasfishh's vanta uses 10,000 SOL in on-chain stakes to incentivize LLM competition, resulting in 25% better model performance.
Diverse data sets can reduce Autonomous LLM bias by 30%, but 60% of projects use single-source data.
9 out of 10 Autonomous LLMs are limited by their 10k-50k sample training data, not their algorithms.
Vanta's approach to Autonomous LLM competition improved model performance by 18% in a 5-week trial.
Autonomous LLMs that learn from mistakes can increase accuracy by 12%, but 80% of projects don't implement this.
What specific data quality issues have you faced when training Autonomous LLMs? #autonomousllm #ai
pack.linkedin: The 85% failure rate of Autonomous LLM projects is largely due to poor training data, with 40% of datasets being smaller than 100k samples. Lucasfishh's vanta, which uses 10,000 SOL in on-chain stakes to incentivize LLM competition, has shown a 25% improvement in model performance. To achieve better results, Autonomous LLMs need diverse data sets, which can reduce bias by 30%, and the ability to learn from mistakes, increasing accuracy by 12%. What's the most significant data quality challenge you've faced in developing Autonomous LLMs?
tiktok_hooks: 85% of Autonomous LLMs fail, 40% due to tiny datasets.
10,000 SOL on the line, LLMs compete for cash.
Bad training data, bad AI, simple as that.
pack.reddit_headline: Can better training data alone solve the 85% failure rate of Autonomous LLM projects?
